message:
refactor(fsqlite-core): use itoa for prepared-direct insert integer encoding

Replaces the hand-rolled stack-buffer integer-to-string encoder in
`Connection::append_prepared_direct_simple_insert_text` with
`itoa::Buffer::format`. Net change: -25 / +9 lines in connection.rs.

The original code allocated a `[u8; 20]` on the stack, walked the
remainder of `integer.unsigned_abs()` digit-by-digit (manual mod/div
loop), prepended `-` for negatives, then ran the resulting byte slice
through `std::str::from_utf8` with an error path that "couldn't really
fail but we had to handle it anyway." The whole machine was reproducing
what `itoa::Buffer::format` does correctly and faster.

Call-site update: the function previously returned `Result<()>` solely
because of the `from_utf8` error branch. itoa's `format` returns
`&str` infallibly, so the function is now `fn(...)` (no Result). The
three call sites in `eval_prepared_direct_simple_insert_concat_chain`
drop their `?` propagation accordingly.

Dependency wiring:
- Workspace `Cargo.toml`: declares `itoa = "1.0"` alongside the other
  serde-stack workspace deps (it sits next to serde_json since itoa
  is used by serde_json under the hood, so the build effectively
  already pulled it in transitively — this just makes our use of it
  explicit).
- `crates/fsqlite-core/Cargo.toml`: adds `itoa = { workspace = true }`
  in the same alphabetical block as `serde_json` and `hashbrown`.
- `Cargo.lock`: a single new `itoa` entry under the fsqlite-core
  package's dependencies (resolves to 1.0.18, which was already in
  the lockfile transitively via serde_json).

Behavioral equivalence: itoa produces the same decimal representation
the old loop did for all i64 values including i64::MIN (where the
old code used `unsigned_abs()` to side-step two's-complement overflow
on `-i64::MIN`; itoa handles this directly). No round-trip test
changes were required.
